original: Second Council of Constantinople (5th ecumenical council) opens
Jews are expelled from Speyer, Germany
English Short Parliament rises
Dutch politician Nicolaas Witsen visits the politically powerful Patriarch Nikon, head of the Russian Orthodox church in Moscow
French ballerina Marie de Camargo aged 16 makes her premiere at Paris Opera Ballets in "Les Caractères de la Danse"
Laurence Shirley, 4th Earl Ferrers, is the last aristocrat hung at Tyburn, for the murder of his steward - held up then as evidence of the rule of law
Russia and Prussia sign the Treaty of Saint Petersburg, ending the Seven Years' War
Smolny Institute forms in St Petersburg for noble girls
Great Britain passes a tax on hair powder - a fee of 1 guinea a year, leading to a decline in its use
Citizenship is denied to Jews of Aargau Canton, Switzerland
Mary Kies is the first woman too be issued a US patent for weaving straw
British attack Fort Ontario, Oswego, New York
American Bible Society organized in New York
King Leopold I of Belgium opens Brussels-Mechelen railway
City-wide fire burns for over 100 hours in Hamburg, Germany
British Commodore James Plumridge attacks Finnish settlements in Gulf of Bothnia, killing civilans and destroying British-owned goods.
New York State Commission on Emigration assumes lease of Castle Garden (now known as Castle Clinton), at the lower tip of Manhattan, to be used for immigration
Alexandria, Virginia - Confederate troops abandon the city
Battle at Williamsburg, Virginia, is inconclusive
French army intervenes in Puebla, Mexico: Cinco de Mayo
Irish boxer Joe Coburn KOs American Mike McCoole in the 67th round in his first defense of Heavyweight C'ship of America in Charlestown, Maryland
Battle between Confederate and Union ships at the mouth of the Roanoke River
Battle of Wilderness, Virginia (Germanna Ford, Wilderness Tavern)
Campaign in Northern Georgia - Chattanooga to Atlanta
Five days of fighting begin at Rocky Face Ridge during the Atlanta Campaign
First US train robbery occurs at North Bend, Ohio
The British and Foreign Society for Improving the Embossed Literature of the Blind adopts Braille as best format for blind people
Dutch 2nd Chamber passes child labor law
Anti-Jewish rioting in Kyiv, Ukraine
The Bay View Tragedy occurs, as militia fires upon a crowd of protesters in Milwaukee, Wisconsin killing seven.
Robert S. Abbott publishes 1st issue of newspaper "Chicago Defender"
Soviet Communist Party newspaper Pravda begins publishing (4/22 OS)
German U-20 captures and sinks Britsih schooner Earl of Lathom
US Marines invade Dominican Republic, stay until 1924
St Louis Browns pitcher Ernie Koob no-hits Chicago White Sox, 1-0 at Sportsman's Park III
German-Latvian peace treaty signed
Italian migrant anarchists Nicola Sacco and Bartolomeo Vanzetti charged with murder of a paymaster at a US shoe factory in Massachusetts, both are later executed
Polish troops occupy Kiev
First ranger for Cleveland Metroparks is hired
Miniature newspaper is published (Brighton Gazette 10 x 13 cm)
After sharing the Polo Grounds with the New York Giants for 10 years construction begins on Yankee Stadium in the Bronx, NYC
Unions terminate Twentse textile strike in the Netherlands
Afrikaans is established as an official language in South Africa
Yankee Everett Scott is benched, ending his 1,307-game playing streak
Sinclair Lewis refuses the Pulitzer Prize for a Novel for "Arrowsmith"
Japan and China sign the Shanghai Ceasefire Agreement, making Shanghai a demilitarized zone
Edward Ravenscroft patents screw-on bottle cap with a pour lip
Italian troops occupy Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
MLB Philadelphia Phillies Harold Kelleher pitcher faces 16 batters in 6th, as Cubs score 12 runs, both marks are NL records off one hurler in a single inning
Norwegian government-in-exile forms in London
2 Fokker employees flee Nazi occupied Netherlands to England
Pulitzer Prize for Drama is awarded to Robert E. Sherwood  for "There shall be no night"
British assault on Diego Suarez, Madagascar
US begins rationing sugar during WW II
US Postmaster General Frank C. Walker invents Postal Zone System
Soviet offensive against Sebastopol, Crimea
Dutch Prime Minister Pieter Sjoerds Gerbrandy announces to the nation on Radio Orange that they are liberated
Mauthausen Concentration camp in Austria liberated by US forces from 41st Reconnaissance Squadron
Netherlands and Denmark liberated from Nazi control
Uprising against occupying SS troops in Prague
Mississippi Valley flooding kills 16 and causes $850M in damage
Pulitzer prize awarded to Robert Penn Warren for his novel "All the King's Men"
First squadron of jet aircraft boards a carrier
Detroit Tigers second baseman Charlie Gehringer is elected in the Baseball Hall of Fame
KGO TV channel 7 in San Francisco, California (ABC) begins broadcasting
Statute of Council of Europe drawn up
"Lucy Does a TV Commercial" (also known as "Vitameatavegamin") episode of "I Love Lucy" premieres, garnering 68% of US television viewers
Pulitzer prize awarded to Herman Wouk for his novel "The Caine Mutiny"
Indian parliament accepts Hindu divorce
US performs nuclear test at Nevada test Site
West Germany is granted full sovereignty by its three occupying powers
Broekster Boys soccer team forms in Damwoude
Jim Bailey (US) runs mile a record 3:58.6 in LA, California
World championships of judo are 1st held in Tokyo
Adolf Schärf (67) is elected President of Austria
KNME TV channel 5 in Albuquerque, NM (PBS) begins broadcasting
Pulitzer Prize for Fiction awarded to James Agee for "Death in the Family"
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll in the Marshall Islands
Separatists riot in Quebec
First large-scale US Army ground units arrive in South Vietnam
Stanley Cup Final, Olympia Stadium, Detroit, MI: Montreal Canadiens earn back-to-back titles; beat Detroit Red Wings, 3-2 in OT for a 4-2 series victory
The Kinks release "Waterloo Sunset" as a single; peaks at #2 on the British charts
US performs nuclear test at Nevada test Site
Race riot in Brownsville section of Brooklyn (NYC)
Alitalia DC-8 crashes west of Palermo Sicily; killing 115
A's release pinch runner Herb Washington, who played 104 games without batting, pitching, or fielding. He did steal 30 bases and score 33 runs.
Pulitzer prize awarded to Michael Shaara for historical novel "Killer Angels"
Train collision at Schiedam, Netherlands, kills 24
Masterpiece Radio Theater begins broadcasting
Voyager 1 flies by Jupiter
Konstantinos Karamanlis is elected for the first time President of Greece
Siege at Iranian Embassy in London ends when the SAS and police storm the building
16th and final MLB pre-season Mayor's Trophy Game prior to inter-league play; NY Mets beat NY Yankees, 4-1 to hold 8-7-1 edge
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Foundation announces Cleveland, Ohio chosen as the site of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum
Congress begins Iran-Contra hearings
Detroit Tigers are 11 games back in AL, but go on to win AL East
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Eugene A. Marino installed as 1st African American Catholic archbishop, in Atlanta, Georgia
ABC Masters Bowling Tournament won by Chris Warren
A riot breaks out in the Mt. Pleasant section of Washington, D.C. after Salvadoran man is shot by police
Labour beats Conservatives in British local elections
North-Yemen air force bombs Aden South Yemen
Last basketball game at Boston Garden; Celtics eliminated from playoffs by Orlando Magic, 95-92
"Married With Children" final episode on Fox TV
Iridium-1 Delta 2 Launch, Successful
Conjunction of Sun, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, and the Moon
The government of Sudan signs an accord with the Sudan Liberation Army
Pilot error causes Kenya Airways Flight KQ 507 to crash immediately after takeoff from Douala International Airport in Cameroon
17 people are killed and 47 missing after a flash flood in Nepal
Japan shuts down its nuclear reactors leaving the country without nuclear power for the first time since 1970
10 people are killed in a church attack in Njilan, Nigeria
5 people are killed after a limousine catches fire in Hayward, California
China announces it will upgrade Ethiopia's infrastructure in an effort to improve a China-Africa strategic partnership
Archaeornithura meemannae, the oldest known prehistoric bird, is discovered
Scientists announce the discovery of the oldest & most distant galaxy known to man, EGS-zs8-1
Fort McMurray wildfires: Canadian province of Alberta declares a state of emergency as evacuation of 80,000 people continues
Electric cigarette explodes killing a man in St. Petersburg, Florida, first death from a vaping product
At least 41 people die when a Russian Aeroflot plane catches fire after an emergency landing at Sheremetyevo Airport, Moscow
King Vajiralongkorn is crowned King of Thailand, first monarch to be crowned in nearly seven decades during a three-day celebration in Bangkok
Oil tanker explosion kills 55 in Niamey, Niger
Violent clashes between Israel and Gaza militants over three days have left 4 Israelis and 23 Palestinians dead
Global confirmed cases of COVID-19 reach 3.65 million, US cases pass 70,000 while the UK becomes the most affected in Europe with 29,427 known deaths
São Luis, in Maranhão state becomes the first Brazilian city to enter lockdown because of COVID-19 as its health services struggle to cope
A week of mass protests and demonstrations against government tax reform and poverty in Colombia leaves 24 dead
Baltimore Orioles pitcher John Means no-hits Seattle Mariners, 6-0 at T-Mobile Park, Seattle
Canada is the first country to authorize the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine for 12-15 year olds
Evidence of Africa's earliest burial, a three-year-old boy, 78,000 years ago, in a cave in Kenya published
The possible eviction of Palestinian families from a Jerusalem neighborhood begins days of unrest in the city, injuring hundreds
Karine Jean-Pierre appointed White House Press Secretary to PresidentJoe Biden, first Black and out LGBTQ person in the role
Sinn Féin, led by Michelle O'Neill, wins the most seats in Northern Ireland elections for the first time with 27 seats to Democratic Unionist Party's 25
WHO study of excess deaths worldwide says 15 million more people have died than normal, far above the official COVID-19 death toll of 6 million
Zimbabwe is in the midst of a severe economic crisis with unemployment at 90%, hyperinflation and a falling Zimbabwe dollar, according to economic experts
Huge floods in Brazil's Rio Grande do Sul state reportedly kill 100 people with 100 still missing, some areas record 300 millimeters (11.8 inches) of rain
Met Gala, "Superfine: Tailoring Black Style," is hosted byAnna Wintour, Lewis Hamilton, Colman Domingo, A$AP Rocky, and Pharrell Williams
Pulitzer Prize for Fiction is awarded to Percival Everett for his novel "James"
Pulitzer Prize for Music is awarded to Susie Ibarra her composition "Sky Islands"
Skype, the pioneering online video calling service established in 2003, is taken offline for good by Microsoft
The Vatican announces the Popemobile will be donated and converted into a mobile health clinic for wounded children in Gaza, Palestine
Kublai Khan, grandson of Genghis Khan, becomes ruler of the Mongol Empire
On his second voyage to the New World,Christopher Columbussights Jamaica and lands at Discovery Bay
KingCharles Isurrenders in Scotland
George WashingtonappointsFriedrich Wilhelm von SteubenInspector General of the Continental Army
French Estates General meets for the first time since 1614 at Versailles, summoned by KingLouis XVI
Jean Baptiste Joseph Delambrecommissioned to measure the meridian between Dunkerque to Rodez to calculate accurate length of the metre
Thomas Carlylebegins his famous lecture series "The Hero as Divinity," later collected in his book "On Heroes, Hero-Worship, and The Heroic in History"
Indian Wars:Sitting Bullleads his band of Lakota into Canada to avoid harassment by the United States Army under Colonel Nelson Miles
Music Hall (now Carnegie Hall) opens in New York City, withPyotr Ilyich Tchaikovskyas guest conductor of the New York Music Society Orchestra
Cy Youngpitches the first perfect game in "modern" baseball as the Boston Americans beat Philadelphia Athletics, 3-0
Eugene Bullardgains his pilot's license from Aéro-Club de France and becomes the 1st African-American military pilot (French Air Service)
US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonmakes Communist Labor Party illegal
Perfume Chanel No. 5 is released by fashion designerCoco Chanel
Dayton teacherJohn T. Scopesarrested for teaching evolution in Tennessee
Detroit center fielderTy Cobbgoes 6 for 6 with 4 runs, 5 RBI and 16 TBs in Tigers' 14-8 win over the Browns at Sportsman's Park III, St. Louis
Dmitri Shostakovich's 1st Symphony premieres in Berlin, Germany
Amy Johnsontakes off - first woman to fly solo from England to Australia
Ethiopian EmperorHaile Selassiereturns triumphantly to Addis Ababa
Mahatma Gandhifreed from prison (again)
World War II: AdmiralKarl Dönitz, leader of Germany after Hitler's death, orders all U-boats to cease offensive operations and return to their bases
Bhumibol Adulyadejcrowned KingRama IXof Thailand in the Royal Palace in Bangkok
GeneralAlfredo Stroessnerleads a military coup in Paraguay, overthrowing the government of President Federico Chávez
Alan Shepardbecomes the first American in space aboard Freedom 7
"West Side Story" soundtrack album goes #1 and stays #1 for 54 weeks, more than 20 weeks longer than any other album
Milwaukee Bucks sign #1 NBA Draft pick, star UCLA center Lew Alcindor (Kareem Abdul-Jabbar)
Pulitzer prize awarded toNorman Mailer(Armies of the Night)
Columbia Records releases "There Goes Rhymin' Simon", the third solo studio album by American singer-songwriterPaul Simon; it features songs "Loves Me Like a Rock", "Kodachrome", and "American Tune"
MLB Cincinnati RedsPete Rosebecomes 14th player to get 3,000 hits
After 66 days on hunger strike, 26-year-old Provisional IRA member and British MPBobby Sandsdies in Prison Maze. Nine more hunger strikers die in the next 3 months.
Mike Tysongets 2nd speeding ticket for drag racing in Albany, New York

National release of "Field of Dreams", a sports fantasy drama film based on W. P. Kinsella's novel "Shoeless Joe", starringKevin Costner,James Earl Jones,Ray LiottaandBurt Lancasterin his final film role
American country singerTammy Wynettehospitalized with bile duct infection
Jacques Chiracwins the French Presidential election for a second term defeating Jean-Marie Le Pen leader of the far-right National Front
George Gallowayis elected as MP for Bethnal Green and Bow in the general election
Tony Blair's Labour Party is re-elected for a third consecutive term in UK general election
American/Columbia Records releases "Home Before Dark", the 27th studio album byNeil Diamond; tops charts in US, UK and NZ
World Snooker Championship, Crucible Theatre, Sheffield: Briton Mark Selby beats countrymanRonnie O'Sullivan18-14, the first of 3 world titles
"Traveller" debut album byChris Stapletonis released (Grammy Award Best Country Album, 2016; Billboard Album of the Year, 2016)
Anna Wintour, the editor of US Vogue, is made a dame by Queen Elizabeth at Buckingham Palace
Childish Gambino [Donald Glover] releases the music video to new single "This is America" to wide acclaim
Former Manchester United Manager SirAlex Fergusonsuffers a brain haemorrhage and is hospitalised; recovers
US PresidentJoe Bidenannounces the US will support temporarily lifting patent protection on COVID-19 vaccines with the WHO
Chinese PremierXi Jinpingvisits Europe for the first time in five years, arriving in France
Panic of 1893 causes a large crash on the NY Stock Exchange
US Great White Fleet arrives in San Francisco
